explicit_text: My name is Steph Bryant, and I am a registered dietitian working primarily in pediatric weight management. I work at the Roxboro Road Pediatrics Clinic, which is part of Duke Pediatrics, helping kids and families manage health and nutrition. During the COVID-19 pandemic, my work shifted between telehealth and in-person visits, and I also took on the role of wellness committee coordinator to support my clinic staff. Balancing my professional responsibilities and personal life during this time has been challenging but rewarding.

explicit_text: I work within Duke University’s pediatric system, specifically in the Duke Healthy Lifestyles clinic housed at Roxboro Road Pediatrics. Duke has been very supportive throughout the pandemic, providing open communication and adapting to telehealth despite initial challenges. Our clinic receives referrals from various parts of Duke and beyond, and the university’s resources have been helpful in managing staff wellness and patient care during these times.

explicit_text: I work at the Roxboro Road Pediatrics Clinic in Durham, which is where I see most of my patients. This clinic is part of Duke Pediatrics and includes a primary care clinic downstairs that refers many children to our pediatric weight management services. During the pandemic, Roxboro Road has been my main workplace, and I also started a wellness committee there to support staff through the challenges we faced.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic drastically changed my work and personal life starting in March 2020. Initially, I shifted to 100% telehealth before returning to in-person clinic visits part-time, all while managing the stress of exposure risks, especially since many of the families I work with are frontline or essential workers. The pandemic also highlighted food insecurity issues among my patients, and I took on additional responsibilities to support clinic staff wellness during this difficult time.

Initially, I shifted to 100% telehealth before returning to in-person clinic visits part-time, all while managing the stress of exposure risks, especially since many of the families I work with are frontline or essential workers. This crisis also highlighted food insecurity issues among my patients, and I took on additional responsibilities to support clinic staff wellness during this difficult time.

entity: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
explicit_text: We have a housemate who is a student at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and also the son of one of my coworkers. When he couldn’t live on campus safely during the pandemic, we offered him a room in our home so he could stay close to the university in case it reopened. This arrangement helped him maintain proximity to UNC while navigating the uncertainties of campus life during COVID-19.
